title: SHARDOR Conical Burr Espresso Coffee Grinder Electric with Precision Timer 2.0, Touchscreen Adjustable Burr Mill with 51 Precise Settings for Home Use, Anti-static, Stainless Steel
description:
About this item
Premium 40 Millimeter Stainless Steel Conical Burr: Designed to minimize grinding heat and protect the essential oils in the coffee bean, creating uniform grounds for optimal flavor extraction.
51 Precise Grind Settings: Adjustable from the finest for Espresso to the coarsest for French Press Grind.
Precision Cup Quantification and Electronic Timer: The cup number can be selected from 1 to 12 cups. Precision Electronic Timer allows you to adjust grind time up to 60 seconds in 0.1 second increments and consistent dose every time.
Easy to Use: Grind the coffee beans directly into portafilter by using portafilter cradle which is a real-time saver.
Easy to Clean: The anti-static technology makes the ground coffee chamber mess-free. The coffee grounds residual cleaner is good for cleaning the residual coffee grounds.
reviews_5s:
This innovative yet simple coffee grinder was a great upgrade from my blade coffee grinder. I am using a Breville Precision brew drip coffee maker and have been desiring a quality burr grinder to pair with it that didn’t cost as much as a professional level coffee grinder to pair with it. I believe I found the perfect match.

This grinder uses same design principles as larger pro grinders just on a smaller scale. The hopper is quite innovative as it has a small bean gate built into the bottom to avoid spilling beans during burr cleaning process (which also has a very clear 14 step instruction near back of the manual). The hopper also included a very curious bellows attachment, which is simple but brilliant to help “puff” grinds out of the burrs for cleaning, but I use at the end of grinding to help maintain a clean burr to reduce the required cleaning.

The operation of the grinder actually helps me portion the right amount for my coffee maker using the same units as the coffee maker (cups of coffee), which eliminates the need for measurement of beans. I just set for the matching amount of water in my coffee maker, select my grind preference and hit start. It’s so great!
Time will tell if this grinder holds up without l breakdown or failure, but for now, I’m really excited about this addition to my coffee station.Play Video
Got a super cheap espresso machine on clearance after Christmas and didn’t know how expensive espresso grinders were. Had I known before, I likely would not have bought an espresso machine at all as I didn’t want to spend a ton of money. The machine came with a pressurized basket which is much less fussy with grind size, but my gut told me that even though the machine is very cheap, I could pull nicer shots if I upgraded to a bottomless portafilter. That would require a grinder though and I wasn’t ready to shell out $200+ on the grinders most people recommend and decided to take a gamble on this one.

I can say that for a beginner, it has been great! For my setup, around 3 is a good grind setting. The grind adjustment is a little finicky as the clicks between each size are loose which doesn’t give me a ton of confidence of what exact setting I’m on. The bellows attachment for the hopper is nice but could be a little more snug as it tends to come unseated pretty often. The cradles for have a loose hold for my portafilter and even with the included dosing funnels, don’t always offer the best seat or hold. Some beans stick to the inside of the hopper and I would imagine the grinder isn’t the best when it comes to retention. Noise isn’t terrible but could be quieter.

However, all of those cons are in my mind heavily outweighed by the overall value. I have choked up my machine at the finest grind settings- which to me is a good indicator that I have plenty of room to move into lighter roasts for espresso down the road. I was initially very hesitant but am happy to say that I have been very impressed with what this grinder delivers for the price!Purchased for college student. Great starter machine for the price. Easy to clean. Grinding is decent. A little loud but to be expected.This Shardor grinder makes more flavorful espresso than the one I’ve been using for the past 15 years. It’s quieter, faster, cleaner and finishes without adding a mess onto the kitchen surface.I didn’t expect much but it works remarkably well & is way quieter than expected. Great buy so far we will see how long it lasts. Going on several months so far & still good.I love the design and the convenience . I don’t don’t have to dump it into a container first and then put it on the scoop . I can use the scoop directly on the grinder which save a lot timeI purchased this Shardor espresso coffee grinder as a wedding gift. The recipient has told me that it works great.This is an amazing little grinder. Multiple settings for however, fine. You may want it to be. I use mine every day and love it.I've had this almost 2 months and its worked very well so far. Good performance at a reasonable price.This is so much quieter than my other burr grinder and so much cleaner!
reviews_1s: digital display touchscreen didn't work well, couldn't set default timers, etc. the back-press portafilter sensor is too sensitive. the result of using this thing was that i kept spilling coffee grounds everywhere. couldn't grind a good shot. returned this and replaced with the $199 breville grinder.I bought this product as new. It came packed as if it were new. However, it had coffee grind residue in the hopper and smelled like coffee beans.I received a used grinder. It had coffee all over and it was just shoved back in its box. Horrible quality check
